title: Decoding approaches for protein and peptide identification
abstract: Methods and systems are provided for accurate and efficient identification and quantification of peptides. In an aspect, disclosed herein is a method for identifying a peptide in a sample of unknown peptides, comprising receiving information of a plurality of empirical measurements performed on the unknown peptides; comparing the information of empirical measurements against a database comprising a plurality of peptide sequences, each peptide sequence corresponding to a candidate peptide among a plurality of candidate peptides; and for each of one or more of the plurality of candidate peptides, generating a probability that the candidate peptide generates the information of empirical measurements, a probability that the plurality of empirical measurements is not observed given that the candidate peptide is present in the sample, or a probability that the candidate peptide is present in the sample; based on the comparison of the information of empirical measurements against the database.
